chunk_summary: This section discusses prevention counseling for viral hepatitis and nutrition risks in India, focusing on chronic liver disease and hepatitis B management, with guidelines from national and international health organizations.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of nutrition in managing chronic liver disease in special populations.
Learn prevention strategies for viral hepatitis in India, including hepatitis B management.
Identify nutritional risks associated with chronic liver disease in pediatric and adult populations.
Explore evidence-based guidelines for long-term outpatient counseling in liver disease.
key_facts: Hepatitis B is a major public health concern in India, with an estimated 40 million chronic carriers (WHO).
The National Viral Hepatitis Control Programme (NVHCP) provides technical guidelines for hepatitis B diagnosis and management.
Nutritional support is critical for children with chronic liver disease, as per NASPGHAN and ESPGHAN joint guidelines.
Malnutrition is a common complication in chronic liver disease, requiring tailored dietary interventions.
WHO emphasizes vaccination as a key preventive measure for hepatitis B.
Long-term outpatient counseling should address both viral hepatitis prevention and nutrition optimization.
reference_quote: The National Viral Hepatitis Control Programme provides technical guidelines for the diagnosis and management of hepatitis B in India (NVHCP, 2023).
document_key_topics: Prevention of viral hepatitis in India
Nutritional risks in chronic liver disease
